description: The Planck constant is a fundamental physical constant[1] of foundational importance in quantum mechanics: a photon's energy is equal to its frequency multiplied by the Planck constant
weight: and the wavelength of a matter wave equals the Planck constant divided by the associated particle momentum.

description: The Boltzmann constant (kB or k) is the proportionality factor that relates the average relative thermal energy of particles in a gas with the thermodynamic temperature of the gas.
